title: @persist
description: The @persist directive preserves elements across page navigations when using wire:navigate, maintaining their state and avoiding re-initialization.

sections.title: #Basic usage
sections.content:
Wrap an element with @persist and provide a unique name to preserve it across page visits:
When navigating to a new page that also contains a persisted element with the same name, Livewire reuses the existing DOM element instead of creating a new one. For an audio player, this means playback continues uninterrupted.
The @persist directive only works when navigation is handled by Livewire's wire:navigate feature. Standard page loads will not preserve elements.

sections.title: #Common use cases
sections.content:
Audio/video players
Chat widgets
Third-party widgets

examples.code:
@persist('analytics-widget')
@endpersist

sections.title: #Placement in layouts
sections.content: Persisted elements should typically be placed outside Livewire components, commonly in your main layout:

examples.code: <!-- resources/views/layouts/app.blade.php --> <!DOCTYPE html><html lang="{{ str_replace('_', '-', app()->getLocale()) }}"> <head> <meta charset="utf-8"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">  <title>{{ $title ?? config('app.name') }}</title>  @vite(['resources/css/app.css', 'resources/js/app.js'])  @livewireStyles </head> <body> <main> {{ $slot }} </main>  @persist('player') <audio src="{{ $episode->file }}" controls></audio> @endpersist  @livewireScripts </body></html>

sections.title: #Preserving scroll position
sections.content: For scrollable persisted elements, add wire:navigate:scroll to maintain scroll position:

sections.title: #Active link highlighting
sections.content:
Inside persisted elements, use wire:current instead of server-side conditionals to highlight active links:
Learn more about wire:current →

examples.code:
@persist('navigation')
Dashboard Posts Users
@endpersist

sections.title: #How it works
sections.content:
When navigating with wire:navigate:
